title: eremophila interstans
intro: Eremophila interstans, also known as the Golden Eremophila or Golden Fountain Bush, is a flowering shrub native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Eremophila interstans include antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which have been shown to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, or used topically in creams and ointments, making it a versatile and valuable addition to traditional medicine.
